title: Task ordering tests fail intermittently in CI due to likely Go map iteration non-determinism
problem: Unit tests in the task ordering module, added approximately 4 days ago, produce intermittent assertion failures in CI (Ubuntu) where tasks come back in an unexpected order. The same tests pass consistently on the author's local machine (macOS). The failures are blocking releases.
root_cause_hypothesis: The task ordering code almost certainly iterates over a Go map (e.g. map[string][]Task or similar) to build its result set without explicitly sorting afterward. Go randomizes map iteration order at runtime, but the degree of observable randomness can vary across OS, hardware, and runtime scheduling. This explains why results appear stable on one developer's macOS machine but scramble in CI on Ubuntu.
reproduction_steps: Check out the task ordering PR merged approximately 4 days ago
Run the task ordering tests in a loop: go test -count=100 -run TestTaskOrdering ./path/to/task/ordering/...
If that doesn't reproduce locally, run with -race flag or on an Ubuntu environment matching CI
Alternatively, inspect the code path for map iteration feeding into the returned slice — the bug will be visible by reading the code
environment: CI: Ubuntu, Go 1.22. Local (reporter): macOS, Go 1.22. Test runner: go test (standard).
impact: Blocking all releases for the team. Every CI run has a chance of spurious failure, eroding confidence in the test suite and slowing the entire development workflow.
recommended_fix: Find the map iteration in the task ordering return path (likely in the grouping/sorting logic added in the recent PR). After collecting results from the map, sort the output slice explicitly using sort.Slice or slices.SortFunc before returning. This ensures deterministic ordering regardless of map iteration order. Verify by running go test -count=100 on the affected tests.
proposed_test_case: Run the existing task ordering tests with -count=1000 to confirm they pass deterministically after the fix. Optionally add a test that explicitly constructs a map with multiple keys, iterates it 100 times, and asserts the output order is stable after the sort step.
information_gaps: Exact PR number or branch name (easily found by searching recent merges in the task ordering module)
Exact file path of the affected tests and production code (discoverable from the PR)
Whether CI uses any test parallelism flags like -parallel beyond Go's defaults (unlikely to be the primary cause but worth confirming)
